title: Blue Origin hot fires its first previously flown booster, prepares for weekend launch
summary: The engine test, called a static fire, saw the company ignite all seven of its BE-4 engines for less than half a minute. Local maritime and aviation hazards suggest a launch could happen as soon as Sunday, April 19.

title: Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding
summary:
Now that the Artemis 2 mission has been successfully completed, it’s worth taking a look at where NASA stands on the role of humans in exploring space and what its path forward should be. Doing this is especially important today, with some people questioning on line whether we can afford the cost of sending humans […]

title: Three ESA-built satellites on show in France
summary: Three Earth observation satellites, developed by the European Space Agency (ESA) with European partners, and due to launch later this year, have completed their functional and environmental tests and are ready to travel to the European spaceport in French Guiana. But first, journalists were invited to have one last look.

title: Voyager to fly private astronaut mission to ISS
summary:
Voyager Technologies will be the third company to fly a private astronaut mission to the International Space Station, gaining experience for its commercial station.
The post Voyager to fly private astronaut mission to ISS appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Aethero orders Titan satellite to advance space-based data center ambitions
summary:
Aethero is preparing to deploy its most powerful computing payload yet this fall, aiming to bring data center-style processing to orbit and expand the scale of AI workloads that can be handled in space.
The post Aethero orders Titan satellite to advance space-based data center ambitions appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: PlanetiQ secures $15 million Air Force STRATFI contract
summary:
COLORADO SPRINGS – Commercial satellite operator PlanetiQ will develop and launch spacecraft equipped with next-generation instruments to gather terrestrial and space weather data with a $15 million U.S. Air Force Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract announced April 16. The STRATFI agreement is “a big indication from the U.S. government that our technology matters and they […]

title: Just opened: five tonnes of science and supplies
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot and NASA astronauts Jack Hathaway, Jessica Meir and Chris Williams take a moment to capture the occasion as they first open the Cygnus NG‑24 cargo spacecraft after its installation on the International Space Station.

title: Eyeing the Richat Structure
summary: The circular geologic feature in northwestern Africa can be hard to recognize from the ground, but it is obvious when viewed from space.
